mnemonics: CREATE: Contract Requires Every Action To Execute
keyPoints: A contract creation transaction is a special type of transaction that has a 'to' field set to the zero address (0x0).
Its primary purpose is to deploy a new smart contract's bytecode onto the blockchain.
The data payload of the transaction contains the compiled bytecode of the smart contract to be executed upon deployment.
commonMistakes: Confusing a contract creation transaction (deploying new code) with a message call transaction (interacting with existing code).
Assuming the contract address is known before the transaction is mined; it is deterministically computed from the sender's address and their transaction count (nonce).
